dashboard_welcome_intro: Hello {name}. Your dashboard is empty — Phase 2 lands games.
dashboard_unauth_intro: Sign in with Google to begin.
empty_games_body: Add your first game card to start tracking. You'll paste your Steam store URL like {url} on the next screen.
empty_items_example_youtube_url: Paste a YouTube video URL like {url} in the box above. Reddit support arrives in Phase 3.
empty_events_body: Log a conference, talk, Twitter post, or Telegram drop. Or paste a tweet URL like {url} on a game page to log it automatically.
empty_audit_body: Sign-ins, key changes, and item edits will show up here. Only you can see this log.
empty_youtube_channels_body: Add a channel handle like {url} to mark videos from that channel as your own (otherwise they're logged as blogger coverage).
empty_keys_steam_heading: No Steam Web API key saved.
empty_keys_steam_body: A Steam Web API key is optional. Without one, the wishlist tracker is read-only — Phase 3 lands the manual-entry form and the Steamworks CSV importer. Get a key at `https://steamcommunity.com/dev/apikey` if you want auto-fetch in Phase 3.
empty_feed_body: Add a data source on the Sources page to start auto-importing content, or paste a one-off URL like `https://youtube.com/watch?v=…` from a game page. Everything you log shows up here, newest first.
empty_feed_filtered_body: Try clearing one of the filters or expanding the date range. The "Inbox" filter shows events you haven't attached to a game yet.
empty_sources_body: Register a YouTube channel, Reddit account, or other social handle to auto-import content as it's published. Example handle: `@RickAstleyYT`. Phase 3 turns auto-import on; Phase 2.1 sets up the registry.
source_kind_phase_twitter_account: Out of v1 scope — Twitter API paid
sources_status_auto_on_pending: Auto-import: ON · Phase 3 will start polling
sources_kind_disabled_tooltip: {kind} — {phase}. Schema is ready; the polling adapter ships in {phase}.
steam_listing_duplicate_active_prefix: This Steam app is already attached to a game —
steam_listing_duplicate_soft_deleted: This Steam app is attached to a soft-deleted game. Restore the game or use a different appId.
games_detail_events_empty: No events for this game yet.
games_edit_description_placeholder: What's this game about? Pitch, target audience, current status…
inbox_badge_tooltip: This event isn't attached to a game yet. Use the "Attach to game" picker on the right.
sources_error_kind_not_yet_functional: {kind} sources will work in {phase}. The schema is ready; the polling adapter isn't.
sources_error_duplicate: You've already registered this data source.
feed_attach_error_game_not_found: That game isn't available. It may have been deleted.
feed_attach_error_already_attached: This event is already attached to "{title}". Detach first if you want to move it.
feed_dismiss_error_not_in_inbox: Only inbox events (no game attached) can be marked not game-related.
ingest_error_malformed_url: That doesn't look like a URL. Paste a full link starting with https://
ingest_error_unsupported_host: URL not yet supported. Add it as a free-form event from the Events page.
ingest_info_reddit_phase3: Reddit support arrives in Phase 3. For now, log this post as a free-form event from the Events page.
ingest_error_youtube_unavailable: Couldn't fetch that video. It may be private, deleted, or region-locked.
ingest_error_oembed_unreachable: YouTube's metadata service is temporarily unreachable. Try again in a moment.
keys_steam_error_invalid: Steam Web API rejected that key. Double-check the value and try again.
keys_steam_error_label_exists: A key with this label already exists. Pick a different label.
error_server_generic: Something went wrong on our side. Try again in a moment.
error_network: Couldn't reach the server. Check your connection and try again.
confirm_game_delete: Soft-delete "{title}"? You can restore it within 60 days from the deleted-games view.
confirm_event_delete: Delete this event? You can restore it within 60 days.
confirm_item_delete: Remove this tracked video? You can restore it within 60 days.
confirm_key_remove: Remove your Steam Web API key? Wishlist auto-fetch in Phase 3 won't work until you save a new key. This cannot be undone.
confirm_key_replace: Replace your Steam Web API key? The previous key will be permanently overwritten. This is recorded in the audit log.
confirm_signout_all: Sign out from every device? You'll need to sign in again everywhere.
confirm_source_remove: Remove "{display_name}"? Existing events stay in your feed but will keep pointing at this retired source. You can restore the source within 60 days from the deleted-sources view.
settings_sessions_blurb: These are the devices currently signed in to your account. Sign out from any session you don't recognize.
settings_theme_blurb: Light and dark themes use your device preference unless you choose one explicitly. Changes apply immediately on every page.
events_detail_phase4_body: Graphs and per-event metric history arrive in Phase 4 with VIZ-01 (LayerChart-driven event detail). For now, head back to your feed.
events_detail_phase4_chart_placeholder: VIZ-01 lands in Phase 4 — view-count and engagement charts will render here once the polling worker ships.
events_edit_games_empty: No games yet. Create one first.
events_edit_games_label: Games (select one or more — leave empty to drop in inbox)
events_edit_standalone_conflict: "Not game-related" is mutually exclusive with attached games. Clear games or the flag.
events_edit_standalone_help: Clear this to attach a game
events_new_url_required: A YouTube URL is required for YouTube video events.
feed_attach_no_games_inline: No games yet — + Add a game
privacy_section_who_we_are_body: Neotolis Game Promotion Diary is operated by an indie game developer. The service is open-source and MIT-licensed; the source code lives at https://github.com/d954mas/neotolis-diary. For all data-protection questions, contact {supportEmail}.
privacy_section_data_collected_body: Identity: your Google email address and your unique Google account identifier. Usage: every API request's IP address and user-agent are recorded in the per-user audit log. User content: game cards, registered data sources, events (videos, posts, conferences), notes, tags — exactly the content you enter. OAuth tokens (Google access + refresh tokens) AND per-user API keys are envelope-encrypted at rest with a per-row DEK wrapped by a KEK loaded from the environment.
privacy_section_lawful_basis_body: We process your data to perform the service contract you initiated by signing in (Article 6(1)(b)). We do not use your data for marketing, analytics, advertising, or training of AI models.
privacy_section_processors_body: Cloudflare Inc. — CDN, TLS termination, DDoS protection, edge caching. Google LLC — OAuth identity provider. Cloudflare R2 — encrypted off-site backups (server-side encryption + bucket-lock retention).
privacy_section_storage_body: Primary database lives on a VPS in the EU. Backups are stored in Cloudflare R2 with server-side encryption.
privacy_section_retention_body: Active accounts: stored for as long as the account is open. Soft-deleted accounts: kept for {retentionDays} days, then purged. Backups: rotating 30-day window via R2 bucket-lock retention. Worst-case latency to physical disappearance: ~{worstCaseDays} days ({retentionDays} active + 30 backup overlap).
privacy_section_rights_body: Article 15 — Right of Access: download every record we have about you via Settings → Account → Export my data. Article 16 — Right to Rectification: edit anything in-app, or email {supportEmail}. Article 17 — Right to Erasure: delete your account from Settings → Account → Delete my account. A {retentionDays}-day grace period lets you restore. Article 20 — Right to Data Portability: same export endpoint; the JSON shape is documented in our public install runbook. Article 21 — Right to Object: email {supportEmail}. Article 22 — No automated decision-making: we do not run AI on your data.
privacy_section_cookies_body: We set one session cookie (Better Auth) for authentication. We do not use analytics cookies, tracking pixels, or third-party advertising.
privacy_section_children_body: The service is intended for adults (18+). We do not knowingly collect data from children. If we learn we have inadvertently received children's data, we will delete it on notification to {supportEmail}.
privacy_section_security_body: Envelope encryption (AES-256-GCM) for at-rest secrets, TLS 1.3 with HSTS in transit, per-tenant audit log, INSERT-only audit trail, source code public on GitHub.
privacy_section_changes_body: Last updated date appears at the top of this page. Material changes are notified via in-app banner.
privacy_section_complaints_body: You can complain to your national Data Protection Authority. The European Data Protection Board lists national authorities at https://edpb.europa.eu/about-edpb/about-edpb/members_en.
privacy_section_contact_body: For privacy questions, exercise of rights, or any data-protection concern: {supportEmail}
terms_section_service_description_body: Neotolis Game Promotion Diary is a self-tracking diary for indie game developers. You log promotion activity (videos, posts, conferences, press) across platforms and the service tracks engagement metrics over time.
terms_section_account_body: Sign-in is via Google OAuth. One developer account per Google account.
terms_section_acceptable_use_body: No spam, no impersonation, no abuse of registered data sources, no automated scraping at quota-busting rates, no reverse-engineering of internal APIs.
terms_section_limits_body: Default limits: {gamesLimit} games, {sourcesLimit} data sources, {eventsLimit} events per rolling 24-hour window. Hitting a limit is not a service failure — contact {supportEmail} if your usage genuinely exceeds these.
terms_section_early_access_body: The service is in early access. Data preservation is best-effort. Major schema changes may force a re-import. The service may be paused with 14 days' notice.
terms_section_open_source_body: The code is MIT-licensed at https://github.com/d954mas/neotolis-diary. The hosted SaaS instance is offered as-is.
terms_section_warranty_body: The service is provided AS IS, without warranty of any kind. The operator is not liable for indirect, special, or consequential damages.
terms_section_termination_body: You may delete your account from Settings → Account at any time. The operator may terminate accounts violating the Acceptable Use clause with 7 days' notice (except in cases of clear ongoing abuse, in which case termination is immediate).
terms_section_changes_body: Last updated date appears at the top. Material changes are notified via in-app banner.
about_intro_body: Neotolis Game Promotion Diary is a free, MIT-licensed self-tracking diary for indie game developers. Replace messy Google Sheets with a structured, secure, query-friendly diary.
